title: Architecture Intake — Path Graph Output
description: Output of qa-path-graph-miner (§6 Step 1.7). Dependency + route graph with per-edge provenance (source_tool + confidence), and changed-file → reverse-reachable → entrypoint → forward-to-sink critical_paths. HONESTY CEILING: static graphs miss dynamic import / reflection / DI / runtime-registered routes / config-driven routes / cross-process edges — those edges are marked confidence:inferred|reduced, NEVER static-proven. Graph tool missing => graph_status=BLOCKED => downstream coverage claims drop to UNVERIFIED (never silent full-coverage). Written to .qa/evidence/<tag>/path-graph.json (+ critical_paths.yaml summary). Consumed by test-plan synthesis + qa-required-layer-gate.js.

graph_status.description: COMPLETE = graph tool ran clean; REDUCED = fell back to AST/regex (lower confidence); BLOCKED = no usable graph tool → downstream coverage UNVERIFIED.

tool.description: dependency-cruiser / madge / jdeps / pydeps / go-list / cargo-modules / roslyn / ast-fallback / regex-fallback

critical_paths.description: Changed-file-reachable entry→sink paths that should gate release.

id.description: Stable id, also used as the critical_release_paths token (recompute-context).

changed.description: true when a changed file is reverse-reachable to this entry.

coverage_status.description: UNVERIFIED when graph_status=BLOCKED/REDUCED — never claim covered without a static-proven edge.
